apiOrSchemaChanges: export interface SynthesisChunk { text: string; wordCount: number; }
export function prepareForSynthesis(text: string, maxLength: number): SynthesisChunk[]
failingTest: N/A — new module, no pre-existing failing test; acceptance criteria define the required test cases
fileMap: apps/web/src/lib/prepare-for-synthesis.ts — create — new composition module exporting SynthesisChunk interface and prepareForSynthesis function
apps/web/src/lib/prepare-for-synthesis.test.ts — create — vitest test suite for all acceptance criteria
fixApproach: Create a new module `apps/web/src/lib/prepare-for-synthesis.ts` that imports and composes the three existing utilities. The function `prepareForSynthesis(text, maxLength)` first normalizes via `normalizeText`, returns `[]` for empty/whitespace input, splits via `chunkText`, then maps each chunk into a `SynthesisChunk` with `wordCount` from the shared helper. Invalid `maxLength <= 0` throws (delegating to `chunkText`'s existing contract). A co-located vitest suite (`prepare-for-synthesis.test.ts`) covers all acceptance criteria: empty/whitespace, single chunk, multi-chunk splitting, per-chunk wordCount correctness, the no-chunk-exceeds-maxLength invariant, and non-positive maxLength throws.
steps: Create apps/web/src/lib/prepare-for-synthesis.ts: import normalizeText/wordCount from text-utilities, import chunkText, define SynthesisChunk interface, implement prepareForSynthesis (normalize → guard empty → chunk → map with wordCount), throw on maxLength <= 0

Create apps/web/src/lib/prepare-for-synthesis.test.ts: vitest suite covering empty/whitespace input returns [], single chunk under limit, multi-chunk split, per-chunk wordCount accuracy, invariant that every chunk.text.length <= maxLength, non-positive maxLength throws
Run npm run test to verify all tests pass
Run npm run build:web to verify no build errors
summary: Add prepareForSynthesis module composing normalizeText + chunkText + wordCount with vitest suite
verificationCommand: npm run test && npm run build:web
